messages.text: After school. I sit in the quiet library corner, stretching. Today was the longest day of my high school life.
By any standard, this is a lucky rom-com scenario. The school idol invited me to her club. But right now I feel nothing like happiness.
What am I so nervous about? The main event is about to begin. I will visit the Game Club, probably join, and become friends with Tendou and other gamers.
Tendou arrives and sits across from me. Then she drops the unexpected news.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Wait. There is one more person coming.
I freeze. One more person? She never mentioned this. Does that mean I was just an afterthought?
A moment ago I thought of myself as the chosen one. Now I feel ridiculous.
About two minutes later, the library door opens. My encounter with the third member begins.
The person who enters is a stunningly handsome boy. The elegant, fine-featured type. My mood hits rock bottom.

messages.name: Tasuku
messages.text: Ah, sorry, Tendou-san. And you must be Amano-kun, right? I am late. I apologize.
He bows his head deeply. His humble attitude is genuine. I can tell immediately — this guy is a good person.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Let me introduce him properly. This is Tasuku Eiichi from class O. Same year as us. I found him at the game store playing a falling-block puzzle game with incredible focus.
Tasuku extends his right hand toward me, a little shy but natural.

messages.name: Tasuku
messages.text: Please take care of me, Amano-kun. I heard about you from Tendou-san. I do not have many friends... so if you would be willing, I would be happy.
Despite the tension in my chest, I take his hand. His grip is warm and firm.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Ah, yes. I am Amano. Nice to meet you.
I acknowledge he is more handsome, more talented, and kinder than me. I feel utterly inferior. But I force a smile.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Well then, let us all go to the Game Club together.
Tasuku and I glance at each other. We both force awkward smiles and answer in unison.
The atmosphere is heavy with unspoken competition. But at least I feel a small relief — he does not seem like a bad person.
